mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand contraction that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series decline concurrently, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient manufacturing output that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansion periods or Rank IC drops below zero when the macro regime is classified as late-cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening economic activity that boosts investor confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of months with positive payroll growth or the signal shows no predictive accuracy in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 250.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0 or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized economic expansion that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when both series trend upward, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a monetary tightening cycle increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive during tightening cycles or the signal predictive accuracy vanishes when the regime is explicitly classified as monetary tightening.
